data.title: ZyVersa Therapeutics files to sell 4.21M shares of common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• ZyVersa Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ZVSA) filed a prospectus to sell 4.21M shares of common stock.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • Filing

data.title: Beyond Oil signs distribution agreement with Mandarin Food Products in Netherlands
data.description:
• Beyond Oil (OTCQB:BEOLF) has signed its first distribution agreement in the Netherlands with Mandarin Food Products, the company said on Friday.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, which was signed March 25, 2025 and came into effect on the same day, Mandarin Foods will market, distribute, and sell Beyond Oil's proprietary oil filtration solution to restaurants, hotels, catering companies, and institutional food providers across the Netherlands.
  • The agreement includes an initial order of 5,400 kg of Beyond Oil's product and establishes a structured purchasing plan for orders that are expected to increase significantly each year.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sunnova Energy International receives non-compliance notice from NYSE
data.description:
• Sunnova Energy International (NYSE:NOVA) said on Friday that it was notified by the New York Stock Exchange that it is not in compliance with NYSE listing standards, which requires listed companies to maintain an average closing share price of at least $1.00 over a consecutive 30 trading-day period.
  • The company said it will closely monitor the closing share price of its common stock and is considering all available options and intends to regain compliance with the NYSE listing standards, including potentially through the consummation of a reverse stock split, subject to approval by the company’s board of directors and shareholders.
  • NOVA shares were down more than 1% in extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: B. Riley Financial receives Nasdaq delinquency notice
data.description:
• B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) received an expected delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq that RILY was not in compliance with Nasdaq as a result of the delayed filing of the company's full year report.
  • RILY must submit a plan within 60 calendar days or June 2, 2025 to address how it intends to regain compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq can grant an exception to up to 180 calendar days from the filing's due date, or until September 29, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • RILY shares fall 1.8% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: AIM ImmunoTech receives NYSE American notice of delisting and appeal
data.description:
• AIM ImmunoTech (NYSE:AIM) said on Friday that it has received notification from NYSE American that it had suspended trading of the company’s common stock and of its determination to commence delisting proceedings due to the low selling price of the company’s common stock. 
  • The company plans to appeal the determination to the exchange’s Listing Qualifications Panel. 
  • The company’s common stock is expected to begin trading on the Pink Open Market on April 7, 2025.

data.title: Chegg receives non-compliance notice from NYSE
data.description:
• Chegg (NYSE:CHGG) said on Friday that it was notified by the New York Stock Exchange that it is not in compliance with NYSE listing standards because the average closing share price of the company’s common stock as of March 31, 2025, was less than $1.00 over a consecutive 30 trading-day period.
  • As required by the NYSE, the company intends to notify the NYSE timely of its intent to regain compliance with the NYSE minimum share price requirement, which may include, if necessary, effecting a reverse stock split. 
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the listing of the company’s common stock, which will continue to be listed and trade on the NYSE during the six-month cure period. 
  • Chegg (NYSE:CHGG) shares were up more than 3% in extended trading.

data.title: SA Asks: Which space stocks are attractive right now?
data.description:
After soaring shortly after the Trump Inauguration, space stocks have had a rough ride, with several down double-digit percentages year-to-date. Despite the turbulence, many analysts still view the sector as attractive for more adventurous investors.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Quad 7 Capital and Michael Del Monte which stocks they were favoring right now.

Quad 7 Capital: There are three space stocks we have dabbled in: Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB), Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) and AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS). We think ASTS has had the best headline news of late and is well positioned to edge higher. In our opinion, RKLB and LUNR are much more volatile and really impacted by mission-related news and contract wins.

The most near-term upside, which is what we focus on primarily, is in LUNR, in our opinion. After the IM-2 mishap, the stock reset, but the outlook for 2025 for revenue and backlog recognition is quite strong. Then we also had the tariff-induced panic slamming the market. In the medium term for LUNR, we like the growth. Any good news on missions or more contract wins will send it soaring again back to double-digits, particularly with the high short interest. We think it’s a good speculative buy in the $6s.

Michael Del Monte: I believe Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) has the strongest competitive moat when considering space exploration as an industry. Companies like AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) and Globalstar (NASDAQ:GSAT) may be competing with Starlink (STRLK) in terms of direct-to-mobile satellite connectivity, making this a more crowded segment. LUNR by far has a very specific niche in developing a lunar satellite constellation with the intent of generating recurring services revenue on a per-minute basis. This is on top of the $4.82B in potential total contract value for developing the constellation.

  • Top Aerospace and Defense Stocks
